topic: Someone finds their college application essay published as an example
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the embossed lettering on the glossy page, a faint smile playing on her lips. It was her essay, the one she’d poured her soul into—a rambling, heartfelt reflection on the quiet joy of watching moss grow in the shade of an old oak. But here it was, perfectly formatted, titled "The Quiet Persistence of Green." It was featured on the university’s admissions portal as an exemplary piece. A warmth spread through Elara’s chest, a light, buoyant feeling that made her want to laugh out loud. She leaned back in her chair, letting the paper rest against her collarbone, inhaling the faint, fresh scent of ink and paper. Everything felt aligned, purposeful, and utterly right.

[story 2]

Liam stared at the screen, his breath catching in his throat. The words on the screen were his own, the narrative about navigating a difficult summer internship, suddenly elevated to the status of a model submission. A slow, wide grin stretched across his face, crinkling the corners of his eyes. He didn't move for a full minute, just absorbing the validation. A deep sense of ease settled over him, smoothing out the usual knot of anxiety in his stomach. He tapped a finger lightly against the screen, a soft, contented hum vibrating in his chest. It was as if a weight he hadn't realized he was carrying had simply dissolved.

[story 3]

When Maya found the file tucked inside a box of discarded application drafts, she didn't even need to read the title. Seeing her personal narrative—the one about learning to play the cello through sheer frustration—displayed prominently as an example for prospective students made her heart feel light, like a helium balloon rising toward the ceiling. She let out a soft, almost silent exhale, a sound of pure satisfaction. Her fingers drifted over the text, lingering on the eloquent phrasing. A serene calm washed over her; the world seemed suddenly sharper, cleaner, and infinitely more promising. She closed the laptop with a gentle click, feeling utterly settled.
